content: I'm trying to decide on my next big content series - I've been streaming speedcubing tutorials but I think I want to branch out into something new.
That's exciting! Your speedcubing content has been so popular. What other areas are you considering exploring?

I've been really into gardening lately - I've got this amazing mushroom foraging spot near Minneapolis that I discovered last fall. I was thinking about doing a series on identifying edible mushrooms safely.
That sounds fascinating! Mushroom foraging is such a niche topic that could really set your channel apart. Have you thought about how you'd structure the educational content?

I was thinking of starting with the basics - identification, safety precautions, and then moving into more advanced topics like cultivation. My wife has been helping me plan it out since she's the one who got me into gardening in the first place.
It's great that your wife shares your interests! Having a co-creator can really enhance the content. How do you balance streaming with family life?

It's definitely a juggling act with two kids, but we make it work. I usually stream in the evenings after they're in bed. Speaking of balance, I've been training for a triathlon too - I'm thinking about documenting that journey as well.
Wow, you're quite the multitasker! A triathlon training series could be really inspiring for your audience. How's your training going so far?

It's going well - I've been swimming at the YWCA downtown and running along the Mississippi River trails. The biking is the hardest part for me, but I'm getting better. I've also been playing a lot of tennis to cross-train.
The Mississippi River trails are beautiful for running! Have you considered doing any location-specific content about Minneapolis? Your local perspective could be really unique.

That's actually a great idea. I could do some videos about the best outdoor spots in the city - the lakes, the trails, the community gardens. I've been volunteering at one near our house and it's been such a rewarding experience.
I love that idea! Community involvement adds such depth to content. How did you get started with the community garden?

My wife and I joined last spring - we wanted to teach the kids about where food comes from. It's been amazing watching them learn about growing vegetables and herbs. Plus, I've gotten really into pottery recently, so I've been making some ceramic garden markers for the plots.
That's such a creative combination! Pottery and gardening go together perfectly. Have you thought about selling your ceramic pieces or just making them for fun?

Just for fun for now - I've been taking classes at a studio in Northeast Minneapolis. It's become my favorite way to unwind after long streaming sessions. I'm thinking about doing a time-lapse of me making a piece and sharing the process with my audience.
Your audience would love seeing that creative process! Behind-the-scenes content is always so engaging. How do you decide which projects to feature on your channel?
